title: alopecurus capillaris
intro: Alopecurus capillaris, also known as queen's grass or foxtail barley, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant. The health benefits of Alopecurus capillaris include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and alleviating digestive problems. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in some herbal remedies for its purported benefits.
